revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 仲神社 |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 22.2 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 47 |経度秒 = 29.4 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）式内社
旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == [[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀された。 ; 主祭神 :* [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 :* [[菅原道真]] == 由緒 == :創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。 : [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 : [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 : [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 : 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「ユースホステル」下車

{{神社 |名称 = 仲神社 |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 22.2 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 47 |経度秒 = 29.4 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）式内社
旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == [[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀された。 ; 主祭神 :* [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 :* [[菅原道真]] == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。 [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「ユースホステル」下車

拝殿 |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）式内社
旧村社 |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧那賀郡那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == [[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀された。 ; 主祭神 * [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 * [[菅原道真]] == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「ユースホステル」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） [[Category:松崎町の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 仲神社 |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）[[式内社]]
[[旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川 (静岡県)|那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 * [[菅原道真]] 江戸時代の[[神名帳考証]]によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「[[宇都志日金拆命|于都斯奈賀命]][[阿曇氏|安曇氏]]に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の[[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]]

{{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|仲神社 (松崎町)}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki) | native_name = 仲神社 (松崎町) | image = Naka-jinja haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki) | coordinates = {{coord|34.753745|138.802826}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 仲神社 |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）[[式内社]]
[[旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川 (静岡県)|那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 * [[菅原道真]] 江戸時代の[[神名帳考証]]によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「[[宇都志日金拆命|于都斯奈賀命]][[阿曇氏|安曇氏]]に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の[[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]]

[[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] {{commons category|Category:Naka-jinja (Matsuzaki)}} [[JA:仲神社 (松崎町)]] {{wikidata link|Q100464312}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|仲神社 (松崎町)|version=103426473|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 仲神社 |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）[[式内社]]
[[旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川 (静岡県)|那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 * [[菅原道真]] 江戸時代の[[神名帳考証]]によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「[[宇都志日金拆命|于都斯奈賀命]][[阿曇氏|安曇氏]]に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の[[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] {{commons category|Category:Naka-jinja (Matsuzaki)}} [[JA:仲神社 (松崎町)]] {{wikidata link|Q100464312}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|仲神社 (松崎町)|version=103426473|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 仲神社 |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = [[事代主|事代主命]]
[[菅原道真]] |神体 = |社格 = （論）[[式内社]]
[[旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川 (静岡県)|那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 * [[菅原道真]] 江戸時代の[[神名帳考証]]によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「[[宇都志日金拆命|于都斯奈賀命]][[阿曇氏|安曇氏]]に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の[[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] ==End Japanese==

[[旧村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]那賀の[[那賀川 (静岡県)|那賀川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[事代主|事代主命]]。[[伊豆国]][[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[事代主|事代主命]] ; 相殿神 * [[菅原道真]] 江戸時代の[[神名帳考証]]によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「[[宇都志日金拆命|于都斯奈賀命]][[阿曇氏|安曇氏]]に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の[[菅原道真]]は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、[[文禄]]以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、[[那賀郡 (静岡県)|那賀郡]]那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） [[文禄]]2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 [[明治]]6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 [[昭和]]34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * [[伊豆急行]][[下田駅]]から[[東海バス]]『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） ==End Japanese== [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]]

{{jalink|旧村社}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}那賀の{{jalink|那賀川 (静岡県)||那賀川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}。{{jalink|伊豆国|Q860928}}{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀村の村社。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} ; 相殿神 * {{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}} 江戸時代の{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「{{jalink|宇都志日金拆命|Q86736058|于都斯奈賀命}}{{jalink|阿曇氏|Q3377806|安曇氏}}に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、{{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 == 由緒 == 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） {{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 == 摂末社 == * 津島神社（本社に合祀） == 祭事 == * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） == 交通アクセス == * {{jalink|伊豆急行|Q5362418}}{{jalink|下田駅|Q5357199}}から{{jalink|東海バス|Q11527778}}『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献・サイト == * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） ==End Japanese== [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]]

{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = （論）{{jalink|式内社}}
{{jalink|旧村社}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}那賀の{{jalink|那賀川 (静岡県)||那賀川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}。{{jalink|伊豆国|Q860928}}{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀村の村社。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} ; 相殿神 * {{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}} 江戸時代の{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「{{jalink|宇都志日金拆命|Q86736058|于都斯奈賀命}}{{jalink|阿曇氏|Q3377806|安曇氏}}に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、{{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） {{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 === 摂末社 === * 津島神社（本社に合祀） === 祭事 === * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|伊豆急行|Q5362418}}{{jalink|下田駅|Q5357199}}から{{jalink|東海バス|Q11527778}}『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献・サイト === * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|旧村社}} |創建 = 不詳 |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 11月3日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|本殿}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] '''仲神社'''（なかじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}那賀の{{jalink|那賀川 (静岡県)||那賀川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}}。{{jalink|伊豆国|Q860928}}{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内小社}}「仲神社」の論社の1つ。旧社格は旧{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀村の村社。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} ; 相殿神 * {{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}} 江戸時代の{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「{{jalink|宇都志日金拆命|Q86736058|于都斯奈賀命}}{{jalink|阿曇氏|Q3377806|安曇氏}}に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、{{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） {{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 === 摂末社 === * 津島神社（本社に合祀） === 祭事 === * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|伊豆急行|Q5362418}}{{jalink|下田駅|Q5357199}}から{{jalink|東海バス|Q11527778}}『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献・サイト === * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

主祭神 * {{jalink|事代主|Q8009611|事代主命}} ; 相殿神 * {{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}} 江戸時代の{{jalink|神名帳考証|Q11588824}}によると、主祭神は現在と異なり「{{jalink|宇都志日金拆命|Q86736058|于都斯奈賀命}}{{jalink|阿曇氏|Q3377806|安曇氏}}に連なるとある」だった[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja 神名帳考証](230枚目)。相殿神の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は、境内社の津島神社に祀られていたが、{{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}以前に本社へ合祀されたもの。 === 由緒 === 創建時期は不詳。「仲」の由来は、{{jalink|那賀郡 (静岡県)|Q11642882|那賀郡}}那賀郷の「那賀」が転じて「仲」となったという。 中古、伊那上神社に合祀され、社号を「高根明神」と称した。旧中川村村社寺明細帳（発行日：大正15年） {{jalink|文禄|Q1373039}}2年（1593年）旧地である現在地に復社、社号を「仲神社」に復称した。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（1873年）8月村社に昇格した。 {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}34年（1958年）8月14日の台風により、社殿が倒壊したが、翌年に拝殿・覆殿、鳥居をコンクリート造で再建した。 境内の那賀生活改善センターは、集会所として利用されている。 === 摂末社 === * 津島神社（本社に合祀） === 祭事 === * 春祭り（2月11日） * 津島神社 例祭日（7月15日） * 八朔祭り（9月1日） * 秋祭り（11月3日） === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|伊豆急行|Q5362418}}{{jalink|下田駅|Q5357199}}から{{jalink|東海バス|Q11527778}}『堂ヶ島』行き乗車、バス停「宮小路」下車 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献・サイト === * 松崎町史資料編 第一集 神社・寺院編（発行者：松崎町教育委員会、発行日：平成5年3月） {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
{{jalink|former village shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)||Naka River}} in Naka, {{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|Naka District}} in {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{jalink|Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{jalink|Azumi clan|Q3377806|Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{jalink|Shimoda Station|Q5357199}} on the {{jalink|Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418}}, take {{jalink|Tōkai Bus|Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q100464312}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Naka Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
{{jalink|former village shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)||Naka River}} in Naka, {{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|Naka District}} in {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{jalink|Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{jalink|Azumi clan|Q3377806|Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{jalink|Shimoda Station|Q5357199}} on the {{jalink|Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418}}, take {{jalink|Tōkai Bus|Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q100464312}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Naka Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
{{jalink|former village shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)||Naka River}} in Naka, {{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|Naka District}} in {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{jalink|Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{jalink|Azumi clan|Q3377806|Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{jalink|Shimoda Station|Q5357199}} on the {{jalink|Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418}}, take {{jalink|Tōkai Bus|Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{jalink|Shimoda Station|Q5357199}} on the {{jalink|Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418}}, take {{jalink|Tōkai Bus|Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q100464312}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Naka Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
{{jalink|former village shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)||Naka River}} in Naka, {{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|Naka District}} in {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{jalink|Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{jalink|Azumi clan|Q3377806|Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{jalink|Shimoda Station|Q5357199}} on the {{jalink|Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418}}, take {{jalink|Tōkai Bus|Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]]

{{wikidata link|Q100464312}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Naka Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}{{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
{{jalink|former village shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{jalink|Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)||Naka River}} in Naka, {{jalink|Matsuzaki|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}. Its deity is {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{jalink|small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|Naka District}} in {{jalink|Izu Province|Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{jalink|Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{jalink|Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{jalink|Azumi clan|Q3377806|Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{jalink|Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{jalink|Bunroku|Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{jalink|Shimoda Station|Q5357199}} on the {{jalink|Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418}}, take {{jalink|Tōkai Bus|Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)||WD=|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. Its deity is {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. Its deity is {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

content == {{神社 |名称 = Naka Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) {{DEFAULTSORT:なかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|名いこなひめのみことしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]][[Category:qq]]

The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]][[Category:qq]]
