The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

content == {{神社 |名称 = Naka Shrine |画像 = [[File:Naka-jinja_haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|WD=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|WD=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|WD=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|WD=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|WD=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|WD=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]

{{Expand Japanese|仲神社 (松崎町)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki) | native_name = 仲神社 (松崎町) | image = Naka-jinja haiden(Matsuzaki).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki) | coordinates = {{coord|34.753745|138.802826}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)'''|仲神社 (松崎町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|45|13.4|N|138|48|10.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|qid=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}Naka 64 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 13.4 |経度度 = 138 |経度分 = 48 |経度秒 = 10.2 |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |神体 = |社格 = (candidate) {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = November 3 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Shizuoka }} [[File:Naka-jinja_honden(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Honden}}]] [[File:Naka-jinja_Torii(Matsuzaki).jpg|thumb|282px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] '''Naka Shrine''' (なかじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Naka River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Naka River}} in Naka, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki|Q1204017|qid=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Its deity is {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. It is one candidate for "Naka Shrine," a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Naka District, Izu|Q11642882|qid=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}} in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. Its former rank was the village shrine of Naka Village in former {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|qid=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ; Associated hall deity * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} According to the Edo-period {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}, the main deity was then different from today, being "{{ill|lt=Utsushi-nagami|Q86736058|qid=Q86736058|lt=Utsushinaga-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}said to be connected to the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|qid=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}}."[https://kokusho.nijl.ac.jp/biblio/100132933/230?ln=ja Jinmyōchō Kōshō] (leaf 230). The associated deity {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} had been enshrined at Tsushima Shrine in the precincts, but was merged into the main shrine before {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|qid=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}}. === Origin === The foundation period is unknown. It is said that "Naka" in the name changed from "Naga" of Naga-gō in {{ill|lt=Naka District, Shizuoka|Q11642882|qid=Q11642882|lt=Naka District|1=Naka district}}. In the medieval period it was merged into Inakami Shrine and called "Takane Myōjin."Old Nakagawa Village shrine/temple detailed ledger (published Taishō 15) In {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|qid=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 2 (1593), it was re-established at the present former site and resumed the name "Naka Shrine." In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it was promoted to village shrine. On August 14, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 34 (1958), a typhoon destroyed the shrine buildings, but in the following year the haiden, cover hall, and torii were rebuilt in concrete. The Naka Life Improvement Center in the precincts is used as a meeting hall. === Sessha and massha === * Tsushima Shrine (merged into the main shrine) === Festivals === * Spring festival (February 11) * Tsushima Shrine annual festival day (July 15) * Hassaku festival (September 1) * Autumn festival (November 3) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Shimoda Station|Q5357199|qid=Q5357199|1=Shimoda Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Izu Kyūkō Line|Q5362418|qid=Q5362418|1=Izukyū Corporation}}, take {{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|qid=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} bound for "Dōgashima," get off at "Miyakōji" === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References and websites === * Matsuzaki Town History Materials, Vol. 1: Shrines and Temples (published by Matsuzaki Board of Education, March 1993) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naka Shrine (Matsuzaki)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsuzaki Town]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
